_readme: Golden dataset for WanderPlan RAG evaluation. `corpus` is a small, hand-curated set of destination passages (standing in for real Wikivoyage/Reddit ingested content) seeded into Qdrant for eval runs. `queries` pairs realistic user-intent queries with the corpus chunk ids that are genuinely relevant (`relevant_ids`). Extend both lists to grow eval coverage — keep relevance judgments honest (don't mark a chunk relevant just because it mentions the destination; it must actually answer the query intent). Used by eval/run_rag_eval.py to compute Precision@k, Recall@k, MRR, and nDCG@k.

corpus.text: The Louvre is best visited right at opening time to avoid the worst crowds around the Mona Lisa; the Denon wing sculpture galleries are almost empty even at midday.
Le Marais has some of the best falafel and vegetarian sandwich spots in the city, especially along Rue des Rosiers.
The metro runs until about 1am on weekdays and 2am on weekends; buy a Navigo Easy card to avoid paper ticket queues.
Budget-friendly nightlife in Paris centers around Bastille and Oberkampf, with cheap wine bars and student-priced cocktails.
The Promenade Plantée, an elevated garden walkway above Avenue Daumesnil, is a quiet alternative to the crowded Champs-Élysées.
The Jardin d'Acclimatation amusement park and the Cité des Sciences science museum are both well suited for children under 10.
Pickpocketing is common around the Eiffel Tower and on RER B trains to the airport; keep bags zipped and in front of you.
Le Bon Marché and the Marché aux Puces de Saint-Ouen flea market are popular for both luxury and vintage shopping.
Senso-ji Temple in Asakusa is most peaceful before 8am; the Nakamise shopping street leading to it opens later and gets crowded by mid-morning.
Standing sushi bars near Tsukiji Outer Market offer high-quality omakase at a fraction of Michelin-starred prices.
The Suica or Pasmo IC card works across nearly all trains, subways, and even vending machines; top up at any station kiosk.
Golden Gai in Shinjuku has dozens of tiny themed bars, many with a seating cover charge of just a few hundred yen.
Yanaka, one of the few Tokyo neighborhoods that survived WWII bombing, has an old-town atmosphere with cat cafes and a traditional cemetery walk.
TeamLab Planets and the Ghibli Museum are the two most requested family attractions, both requiring advance timed tickets.
Tokyo is very safe even late at night, but earthquake drills are common — familiarize yourself with hotel evacuation routes.
Nakano Broadway and Akihabara are the two best districts for anime figures, retro games, and manga cafes.
Tegalalang rice terraces near Ubud are best visited at sunrise before the tour buses arrive around 9am.
Warungs (small family-run eateries) in Ubud serve nasi campur and babi guling far cheaper and often tastier than resort restaurants.
Renting a scooter is the most flexible way to get around, but an international driving permit is technically required and checkpoints do occur.
Canggu and Ubud both have a strong coworking scene, with Dojo Bali and Outpost being the most popular spaces for long-stay remote workers.
Sidemen Valley offers the rice-terrace scenery of Ubud without the crowds, and is a good base for slower-paced trips.
Rip currents are dangerous at several south coast beaches like Kuta; swim only between the flagged patrol areas.
Ubud has dozens of yoga studios and traditional Balinese massage spas, with Yoga Barn being the most established for drop-in classes.
Seminyak and Canggu beach clubs like Potato Head and Finns draw a lively sunset crowd, with cover charges usually waived before 5pm.
Booking a timed entry ticket for the Colosseum online in advance avoids the multi-hour queue at the entrance.
Trastevere's back streets have trattorias serving cacio e pepe and carbonara at local prices, away from the tourist-priced spots near the Pantheon.
Validate paper bus and metro tickets in the yellow machines before boarding — inspectors do fine tourists for unvalidated tickets.
The Aventine Keyhole, a small gate on Aventine Hill, frames a perfectly hidden view of St. Peter's dome through a hedge tunnel.
The Bioparco di Roma zoo and Explora children's museum are good options for a lower-walking day with young kids.
Scam artists near major landmarks sometimes pose as 'free' bracelet vendors or petition signers as a distraction for pickpocketing.
Testaccio has a mix of clubs and casual bars popular with locals rather than tourists, especially around Monte Testaccio.
Via del Corso and the Monti neighborhood both offer shopping, with Monti favoring small independent and vintage boutiques.
The 9/11 Memorial and Museum requires timed tickets that sell out days in advance during peak season.
Halal carts and pizza-by-the-slice spots in Midtown are the fastest, cheapest lunch options between sightseeing stops.
A 7-day unlimited MetroCard (or OMNY tap card) pays for itself after about 13 subway or bus rides.
The Elevated Acre, a small rooftop park near Wall Street, has skyline and harbor views with almost no tourists.
The American Museum of Natural History and Central Park Zoo are the most reliably kid-friendly full-day options.
The subway is generally safe, but avoid empty end-of-platform areas late at night and stay near the conductor's car.
The Lower East Side and Bushwick have the city's most active indie bar and live-music scene, cheaper than Manhattan clubs.
WeWork locations are common, but independent cafes in Williamsburg and the East Village are popular unofficial coworking spots with good wifi.
